bio_paragraphs: Before picking up a machine, Jeff spent years working as a mental health therapist — sitting with people through hard conversations, paying close attention, and learning how to hold space for someone else's story. That same attention shows up in the tattoo chair now: listening to what a piece actually means to someone before a single line goes down.

Jeff started tattooing in 2025, training through structured coursework and hours of self-study before landing his first apprenticeship at a local shop. He's currently building his craft under the mentorship of an experienced artist, with a heavy focus on clean, confident linework as the foundation everything else gets built on.

His style draws from Nordic and Germanic folklore and knotwork, blackwork, and American traditional — bold, graphic, built to hold up over decades, not just look good on day one. Color work is on the horizon as his linework fundamentals lock in.
Outside the shop, Jeff trains as a strongman athlete, is active in the Bearded Villains brotherhood, and leans into Norse-inspired ritual and imagery — all of which bleed into the work he creates.
values: Fundamentals first — clean linework before flashy shortcuts
Honest communication about what's realistic for size, placement, and healing
Every piece gets full attention, whether it's a $100 flash or a custom project
A calm, judgment-free chair — bring your story, not just your idea
